utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items required in the final format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all details, including costs: 1) health_coverage: It lists 'Wellness Programs Add-on: €200/month' and references a bundled €950/month company contribution covering health, property, and travel. However, it does not clearly separate or itemize the health insurance coverage details (policy name, scope) or the portion of cost attributable to health from the bundle; still, it provides a cost for the wellness add-on and mentions coverage components (global executive health, family mental health counseling). Partially specified but includes costs; arguably acceptable as a rough outline. 2) property_coverage: 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage: €90/month' with clear cost; coverage scope minimal but cost present. 3) travel_coverage: Multiple plans with individual monthly costs and a total (€295/month); sufficiently itemized with costs. 4) additional_services: 'Business Travel Support Add-on: €120/month' plus note on company benefits; has cost. 5) total_budget: '€1,675/month' provided as overall monthly spend. Given the instruction that items must be fully specified with all details, the health_coverage lacks explicit core policy cost breakdown separate from bundle and details of policy terms, and the total budget may double-count because it cites a bundle without breakdown. However, the format only mandates 'with costs' for the first four and a 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown' for total_budget. The total_budget lacks an annual figure but has a complete monthly total. Therefore, all 5 items are present with costs, though some details are thin; still they meet the minimum specified fields.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: From the final coverage plan: (1) Health coverage lists 'Wellness Programs Add-on' which matches Health Enhancement Services -> Wellness Programs with rating 10. (2) Property coverage lists 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage' which matches Property Protection -> home_content -> PrimeHome Premium Coverage with rating 10. (3) Travel coverage lists 'EliteTravel Premium Plan' which matches Mobility Coverage -> travel_insurance -> EliteTravel Premium Plan with rating 10; it also lists 'VitalPlus Standard Travel Plan (Lucas)' which matches Mobility Coverage -> travel_insurance -> VitalPlus Standard Plan with rating 6; it lists 'InsurePlus Standard Plan (Isabella)' which matches Mobility Coverage -> travel_insurance -> InsurePlus Standard Plan with rating 6; and 'Adventure Travel Coverage Add-on (Isabella)' which matches Travel Enhancement Services -> Adventure Travel Coverage with rating 6. (4) Additional services lists 'Business Travel Support Add-on' which matches Travel Enhancement Services -> Business Travel Support with rating 10.

final_package_text:

    "health_coverage": "Wellness Programs Add-on: €200/month (Note: Company Benefits Package also includes global executive health coverage and family mental health counseling as part of a bundled €950/month contribution covering health, property, and travel.)",
    "property_coverage": "PrimeHome Premium Coverage: €90/month",
    "travel_coverage": "EliteTravel Premium Plan: €110/month, VitalPlus Standard Travel Plan (Lucas): €55/month, InsurePlus Standard Plan (Isabella): €50/month, Adventure Travel Coverage Add-on (Isabella): €80/month (Total Travel: €295/month)",
    "additional_services": "Business Travel Support Add-on: €120/month (Note: Company Benefits Package also includes Golf and recreation accident coverage and Executive liability protection as part of a bundled €950/month contribution covering health, property, and travel.)",
